reasoning: The informal statement says no order turns ℂ into an ordered field. In modern Mathlib, "ordered field" on a type K is `[Field K] [LinearOrder K] [IsStrictOrderedRing K]` (see the deprecation note in Mathlib/Algebra/Order/Field/Defs.lean). Since ℂ is already a `Field`, asserting that ℂ cannot be made into an ordered field is equivalent to asserting ¬∃ (LinearOrder ℂ) making `IsStrictOrderedRing ℂ` hold. The `let _ : LinearOrder ℂ := inst` step ensures the provided order is used when elaborating `IsStrictOrderedRing ℂ` (which needs a `PartialOrder`, available from `LinearOrder`). The use of `LinearOrder` rather than `PartialOrder` matches the standard definition of an ordered field (total order). Therefore the Lean signature faithfully captures the informal claim.
